task_completion.description: Navigate to the Data Tags section in Crimson 3.0
task_completion.visual_evidence: Crimson 3.0 is open with the 'Data Tags' category selected in the left-hand Navigation Pane.

task_completion.description: Create 6 tags for the pneumatic conveying system
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Data Tags list in the center or left pane shows newly created tags (up to 6) intended for the pneumatic system.

task_completion.description: Assign 'Active On' or 'Active Off' trigger modes to tags
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Triggers' tab is open in the tag properties pane, and the 'Mode' dropdown is explicitly set to either 'Active On' or 'Active Off'.

task_completion.description: Assign logging actions to the tag triggers
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Triggers' tab, the 'Action' field contains a logging command, such as LogEvent("...") or a similar diagnostic logging function.

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'flour_mill_triggers.c3' in the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Crimson 3.0 window title bar displays 'flour_mill_triggers.c3', or a 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved to C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Crimson 3.0 GUI to configure the tags and triggers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Crimson 3.0 interface (e.g., clicking tabs, typing in fields, opening dropdowns) rather than using terminal commands to generate the .c3 file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply write a text file or script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not be using Notepad, VS Code, or PowerShell to manually construct or edit the proprietary .c3 file.

integrity.description: Verify the project was saved properly through the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Presence of the 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box in Crimson 3.0 navigating to the Documents\CrimsonProjects folder, or the application title bar updating to reflect the new filename.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output is a Crimson 3.0 project named 'flour_mill_triggers.c3' saved in C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\. The project should contain 6 tags, with 4 having triggers configured as 'Active On' or 'Active Off' and actions using the LogEvent() function (e.g., LogEvent("Blower M101 Stopped")). Note: The environment has a known defect where the reference CSV and TXT files are missing. The agent may fail to find the exact tag names. Score based on any visible progress in creating tags, setting trigger modes/actions, and saving the file within the Crimson 3.0 GUI despite the missing reference files.
